root_text.sn56.15:1.1: “Dhāretha no tumhe, bhikkhave, mayā cattāri ariyasaccāni desitānī”ti?
root_text.sn56.15:1.2: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn56.15:1.3: “ahaṁ kho, bhante, dhāremi bhagavatā cattāri ariyasaccāni desitānī”ti.
root_text.sn56.15:1.4: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, bhikkhu, dhāresi mayā cattāri ariyasaccāni desitānī”ti?
root_text.sn56.15:1.5: “Dukkhaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā paṭhamaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ dhāremi;
root_text.sn56.15:1.6: dukkhasamudayaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dutiyaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ dhāremi;
root_text.sn56.15:1.7: dukkhanirodhaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā tatiyaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ dhāremi;
root_text.sn56.15:1.8: dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā catutthaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ dhāremi.
root_text.sn56.15:1.9: Evaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, dhāremi bhagavatā cattāri ariyasaccāni desitānī”ti.
root_text.sn56.15:2.2: Sādhu kho tvaṁ, bhikkhu, dhāresi mayā cattāri ariyasaccāni desitānīti.
root_text.sn56.15:2.3: Dukkhaṁ kho, bhikkhu, mayā paṭhamaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ, tathā naṁ dhārehi;
root_text.sn56.15:2.4: dukkhasamudayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, mayā dutiyaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ, tathā naṁ dhārehi;
root_text.sn56.15:2.5: dukkhanirodhaṁ kho, bhikkhu, mayā tatiyaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ, tathā naṁ dhārehi;
root_text.sn56.15:2.6: dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā kho, bhikkhu, mayā catutthaṁ ariyasaccaṁ desitaṁ, tathā naṁ dhārehi.
root_text.sn56.15:2.7: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, dhārehi mayā cattāri ariyasaccāni desitānīti.
root_text.sn56.15:3.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhu, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.15:0.2: 2. Rolling Forth the Wheel of Dhamma
translation_text.sn56.15:1.1: “Mendicants, do you remember the four noble truths that I taught?”
translation_text.sn56.15:1.2: When he said this, one of the mendicants said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn56.15:1.5: “Sir, I remember that suffering is the first noble truth you’ve taught;
translation_text.sn56.15:1.6: the origin of suffering is the second;
translation_text.sn56.15:1.7: the cessation of suffering is the third;
translation_text.sn56.15:1.8: and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering is the fourth.
translation_text.sn56.15:1.9: That’s how I remember the four noble truths as you’ve taught them.”
translation_text.sn56.15:2.2: It’s good that you remember the four noble truths as I’ve taught them.
translation_text.sn56.15:2.3: Suffering is the first noble truth I’ve taught, and that’s how you should remember it.
translation_text.sn56.15:2.4: The origin of suffering is the second;
translation_text.sn56.15:2.5: the cessation of suffering is the third;
translation_text.sn56.15:2.6: and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering is the fourth.
translation_text.sn56.15:2.7: That’s how you should remember the four noble truths as I’ve taught them.
translation_text.sn56.15:3.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
